concerns.text: The 'Wan2.1 (Text-to-Video)' citation points to a LICENSE.txt file (`.../data/LICENSE.txt`) rather than the model weights or architecture definition required for generation. A license file cannot serve as the data source for the Wan2.1 model inference described in the plan. The plan must cite the actual model repository or weight file URL (e.g., from HuggingFace model hub) to be executable.

The 'Wan2.1 (Training Data)' and 'Wan2.1 (Test Set)' citations point to specific parquet/zip files that appear to be generic or unrelated to robotic manipulation (e.g., 'human-preferences', 'ff7_wan21_dataset01'). The plan requires prompts and validation data specifically for 'robotic manipulation' (grasping, pushing, lifting). Using generic video datasets for prompt generation or baseline comparison will not yield the specific domain data required to test the hypothesis. A domain-specific robotic manipulation dataset or prompt source must be cited.

The 'PyBullet (Visual Data)' and 'PyBullet (Episode Data)' citations point to datasets (e.g., 'gym_pybullet_drones', 'pybullet_ring_task') that are specific to drone flight or ring tasks, not the 'robotic manipulation' (grasping, pushing) tasks defined in the User Stories. The filter validation requires ground-truth trajectories for manipulation tasks. Using drone or ring-task data to calibrate a filter for robotic arm manipulation is an inappropriate dataset match and will likely result in a filter that fails to detect relevant physical inconsistencies in the target domain.
